question: When solving an equation, why must you perform the same operation on both sides of the equals sign?
options: To double the values of both sides
To maintain the balance of equality so both sides remain equal
To make the equations easier to graph
answerText: To maintain the balance of equality so both sides remain equal
explanation: An equation represents a balanced scale. If you do something to one side (e.g. add 5 or divide by 2), you must do the exact same thing to the other side to keep the equation balanced and true.
